Try HARD to REFUTE this proposed change to the POLARIS pipeline. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Read code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel if needed.

CHANGE: Question-driven scope section (sectoral coverage) via the facet skeleton (C5)
FILE/SEAM: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:1003-1013 (_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM), gates at :947-996 (PG_FACET_OUTLINE, PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON, both default OFF); _parse_outline already accepts required_sections (:1773) :: Comprehensiveness carries an industry-scope criterion (0.25 of the 0.29 dimension) and the task prompt itself says 'affecting various industries' — we ship ZERO industry-organised content. The facet skeleton that could host it exists and is dark.
WHAT: Arm PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 + PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1, plus a new gated addendum PG_FACET_SCOPE_DIMENSION (default OFF) appended to _FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM: 'If the research question names a scope dimension (industries/sectors, regions, populations, time periods), include exactly ONE thematic body organized ALONG that dimension, with one H3 per sub-scope the evidence genuinely covers; assign each sub-scope its supporting evidence IDs; never fabricate a sub-scope the evidence cannot ground.' Question-driven, no hardcoded topic — generalizes across the 100 tasks because it keys off the task's own text, avoiding the overfit trap named in the brief.
CLAIMS: +0.8 to +1.5 overall. This is the single largest zero-to-one criterion: we currently score a structural zero on a 0.25-weight comprehensiveness criterion. on comprehensiveness (0.29; industry-scope criterion) + instruction-following (0.25; 'various industries' is a graded instruction) (w=1.2)
FAILURE MODE THEY ADMIT: (a) Evidence pool may lack sector-tagged rows — the section renders thin or gap-stubs, and a gap stub on a prominent section is worse than absence; the outline-only test catches thin assignment before compose. (b) Flipping the whole outline to facet mode changes EVERY section title simultaneously — a confounded A/B; must be measured as its own arm. (c) Facet mode is the least-exercised outline path; parser fallback to generic-6 silently discards the whole change (detectable in the outline log).
NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
SCORE-INVISIBILITY: RACE strips citations and the bibliography, and judges the CLEANED prose against a reference that scores 0.5000. Would the judge actually SEE and REWARD this change? Recall the prior insight-directive A/B went the WRONG way (0.4094 vs 0.4447 control) — explain why this would not repeat.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

# O1 (I-deepfix-001 #1344 — facet-driven outline): the live non-clinical outline was
# HARD-CAPPED to a fixed 6-title generic allow-list (Background / Key Findings / Evidence
# and Analysis / Comparative Assessment / Implications / Limitations) AND truncated to 6
# sections. Every distinct facet of a 20-facet question collapsed into 6 generic buckets —
# the container the F/D coverage workstreams all hit. This flag UNLOCKS the container for
# the NON-clinical path only: section TITLES and COUNT emerge from the evidence's real facet
# structure (one topical section per distinct facet the evidence supports), bounded only by
# real evidence-bearing facets — never a target. §-1.3: the truncate-to-6 is a CAP being
# REMOVED, not a knob being added; breadth EMERGES. Clinical/unknown stay byte-identical
# regardless of this flag (the proven Efficacy/Safety/... set governs clinical safety).
# DEFAULT-OFF (byte-identical) — the beat-both run config turns it on. LAW VI: env-tunable,
# read at CALL time so it is unit-testable via monkeypatch.
_FACET_OUTLINE_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE"
_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS"
_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS"
# Compute-safety ceiling ONLY (NOT a quality target, §-1.3): protects against a pathological
# outline that emits hundreds of near-duplicate facet titles (a section is billed per title).
# Generous; keyed to real evidence-bearing facets. Hitting it does NOT fail the outline (the
# facet plan is kept, not collapsed to the generic-6 fallback).
_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT = 40
# Below this many VALID facet sections the outline is treated as an EMPTY decomposition and
# the caller falls through to the generic-6 fallback. Default 1: a single well-grounded facet
# section is accepted (breadth emerges); 0 valid sections => generic-6 fallback. NEVER a floor
# that pads a thin outline up to a count.
_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT = 1


def _facet_outline_enabled() -> bool:
    """DEFAULT-OFF flag read at call time (monkeypatch/env-testable). ON widens ONLY the
    non-clinical outline to topical facet sections; clinical/unknown is byte-identical."""
    return os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
    )


def _facet_outline_active_for_domain(domain: str | None) -> bool:
    """True iff the facet-driven outline should govern THIS report: flag ON AND the domain is
    non-clinical (clinical/unknown always keep the proven fixed section set)."""
    if not _facet_outline_enabled():
        return False
    return str(domain or "").strip().lower() not in ("", "clinical")


# GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (mission STEP 2 — synthesis-enabling, topic-DRIVEN structure).
# The bare facet outline emits a flat BAG of thematic sections with no intro, no cross-study
# synthesis, and no conclusion/gaps — the exact SHALLOW-STRUCTURE defect that costs INSIGHT and
# COMPREHENSIVENESS on RACE. This flag wraps the emergent thematic facets in the standard
# research-report skeleton (intro/overview -> thematic bodies -> cross-study synthesis &
# contradictions -> conclusions & research gaps). It is a GENERAL structural improvement that
# applies to ANY non-clinical topic (AI/labor, finance, ecology, ...); it hardcodes NO topic,
# NO benchmark prompt, and NO thematic title (those still EMERGE from the evidence facets). The
# four structural ROLES are the only fixed part — a standard scholarly report skeleton. Read at
# CALL time (env/monkeypatch-testable). DEFAULT-OFF => the bare facet prompt (byte-identical).
_FACET_SKELETON_ENV = "PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON"


def _facet_skeleton_enabled() -> bool:
    """DEFAULT-OFF flag (read at call time). ON wraps the facet outline in the general
    research-report skeleton (intro / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusion+gaps).
    Only takes effect where facet mode is already active (non-clinical); clinical stays untouched."""
    return os.getenv(_FACET_SKELETON_ENV, "0").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "", "false", "no", "off",
    )


# The skeleton addendum appended to OUTLINE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_FACET when _facet_skeleton_enabled().
# Structure-ONLY: it constrains the ORDER and the presence of four structural roles; the thematic
# body TITLES and COUNT still emerge from the evidence. Topic-agnostic by construction.
_FACET_SKELETON_ADDENDUM = """

GENERAL RESEARCH-REPORT SKELETON (structure the plan as a COMPLETE report, not a flat bag of topics):
Order the sections so the report reads as a coherent scholarly review with these FOUR structural roles present in EVERY report, regardless of topic:
  1. OPEN with exactly ONE framing/overview section that orients the reader to the question, the scope, and the body of evidence (a good title names the subject, e.g. "Introduction and Scope of the Evidence" or "Overview of <subject>"). Assign it broad, high-level or foundational evidence.
  2. THEN the THEMATIC BODY sections — one per distinct facet the evidence genuinely supports (as instructed above). These titles EMERGE from the evidence; there is no fixed menu and no fixed count.
  3. Include exactly ONE cross-cutting SYNTHESIS section that reads ACROSS the thematic bodies and surfaces where studies AGREE, where they CONTRADICT each other, and how strong/consistent the overall evidence is (e.g. "Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions"). Assign it the evidence whose findings converge or conflict.
  4. CLOSE with exactly ONE section covering CONCLUSIONS and open RESEARCH GAPS (e.g. "Conclusions and Research Gaps"): what the evidence collectively supports, and what remains unresolved or under-studied.
The four ROLES (open / thematic bodies / synthesis+contradictions / conclusions+gaps) are REQUIRED; the thematic-body titles are free to emerge from the evidence. Each section — including the four structural ones — still needs at least 2 genuinely supporting evidence IDs; assign the relevant rows, never fabricate a section the evidence cannot ground."""


def _facet_outline_max_sections() -> int:
    """Compute-safety ceiling for the facet outline (read at call time). Not a target."""
    try:
        v = int(os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_ENV, _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        v = _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT
    return v if v > 0 else _FACET_OUTLINE_MAX_SECTIONS_DEFAULT


def _facet_outline_min_sections() -> int:
    """Minimum VALID facet sections before falling through to the generic-6 fallback (read at
    call time). Floored at 1 — a single grounded facet section is a valid emergent outline."""
    try:
        v = int(os.getenv(_FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_ENV, _FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        v = _FACET_OUTLINE_MIN_SECTIONS_DEFAULT
    return v if v >= 1 else 1

the density guard exists to prevent. The mechanism refused to pad. Good.

themes the judge had already deleted, so _derive_theme_coverage_sections clusters
the PRE-JUDGE evidence. Harmless here (the low-salience skip caught the junk), but

arm tonight has been n=1 and I have twice had to reason about noise with no
variance estimate. Rank10 supplies one. It will be read as a variance probe, and
the Rank9-vs-Rank10 gap is the noise floor for every claim tonight.

input.reasoning:
REFUTED — the proposal's central factual premise is false, and its residual delta is a lever this repo already measured to zero.

1) THE SEAM IS NOT DARK. The proposal says "the facet skeleton that could host it exists and is dark" and its WHAT is "Arm PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 + PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1". Both flags are ALREADY ON in every scoring run. /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:516-517 does `os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE", "1")` / `os.environ.setdefault("PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON", "1")`, and run_gate_b.py force-PINS PG_FACET_OUTLINE in the winner slate (:1291, :2048, :2377, :3967, :4466). The corpus domain is `workforce` (non-clinical), so facet mode is active. Proof from the live artifact: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/rank11_final_compose/multi_section_outline.json is exactly the skeleton — Introduction and Scope of Evidence -> 6 emergent thematic bodies -> Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions -> Conclusions and Research Gaps. The author read the module-level defaults (:947-996) and never checked the run config. Consequences: (a) half the change is a NO-OP; (b) their admitted failure mode (b) — "flipping the whole outline to facet mode changes EVERY section title, a confounded A/B" — is fiction, nothing flips; (c) their failure mode (c) — "facet mode is the least-exercised outline path, parser fallback to generic-6 silently discards the change" — is backwards: facet mode is the ONLY exercised non-clinical path and produced every score from 0.4317 to 0.4447. A proposal that mis-describes its own seam in three of its own risk clauses has not been run against the pipeline.

2) THE RESIDUAL DELTA (one prompt sentence, PG_FACET_SCOPE_DIMENSION) IS A LEVER ALREADY MEASURED TO ZERO. The facet system prompt (multi_section_generator.py:1662) ALREADY offers "Sectoral Productivity Effects" as a literal exemplar title, and the RQ text ("affecting various industries") is already in the planner's context. The planner has had every opportunity, across every run, and never emits a sector section. Why is answered by Rank10 (e87f52e): PG_OUTLINE_THEME_FLOOR — a purpose-built mechanism that clusters the corpus and seeds a section per under-covered theme — was armed and promoted ZERO sections. "This corpus honestly decomposes into ~9-10 salient themes"; the uncovered clusters it skipped were 169 rows of writing/students/marketing/books and 54 rows of legal/paralegal/law, "exactly the alien rows the judge deletes." The proposed addendum is the same lever with a different trigger, aimed at the same exhausted supply.

3) MY OWN EVIDENCE AUDIT CONFIRMS THEIR FAILURE MODE (a) IS THE ACTUAL OUTCOME. Of 997 rows in data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json, only 273 contain any genuine sector term (word-boundary: manufacturing/healthcare/retail/banking/insurance/construction/agriculture/transportation/logistics/customer service/...) in statement+direct_quote. The topic judge (outputs/rank11_final_compose/topic_judge_dispositions.json, 507 off-subject-deletable) kills 122 of those 273. The 151 survivors are not sector studies — they are the SAME general "future of work" PDF chunks (Brookings, Weizenbaum, NBER Generative AI at Work, LexisNexis) already powering the existing sections, which merely MENTION a sector in passing. A forced "Industry-Specific Impacts" section therefore re-milks documents already cited elsewhere, and its sector-specific claims must still clear verify_sentence_provenance span-grounding + numeric legs. Expected output: thin, hedged, generic prose or a gap stub on a prominent section — the outcome the proposers themselves concede "is worse than absence." Rank9 (fb228dd) already showed oversized menus drive milking, not breadth.

4) IT IS NOT A STRUCTURAL ZERO, SO THE HEADROOM IS SMALLER THAN CLAIMED. outputs/rank11_final_compose/report.md already carries ~30 sector references (manufacturing x4, healthcare x3, retail x3+1, industries x3, sectoral x3, sector x5, construction, finance) distributed across the thematic bodies. RACE criterion "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" (0.25 of comprehensiveness 0.29 = 7.25% of total) asks whether the review "examines the restructuring impact across a diverse range of relevant industries" — we score partial credit, not zero. The claim "single largest zero-to-one criterion" is unverified and false as stated.

5) THE CLAIMED EFFECT IS BELOW THIS REPO'S OWN MEASURED NOISE FLOOR. +0.8 to +1.5 (i.e. +0.008..+0.015 on the 0-1 RACE scale) is smaller than the judge variance the repo already established by re-scoring the SAME artifact: 0.4447 -> 0.4291, Δ0.016 (OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md:306). At n=1 per arm — the flywheel's actual regime, and the thing Rank10 was ultimately used to bound — this arm cannot be distinguished from noise. It is unbankable by construction.

6) SCORE-INVISIBILITY LENS (the requested attack). Being fair first: an H2 heading DOES survive RACE's citation-stripping, so unlike the writer-prose insight-directive, a heading like "Sectoral Impacts of AI" would literally be visible to the judge. That is the proposal's one real asset. It is not enough. The insight-directive A/B (0.4094 treatment vs 0.4447 control) failed by a mechanism that reproduces exactly here: a plausible, criterion-aligned directive injected directed-but-thin content, which displaced denser content, and RACE punished the whole report. Here the target criterion is worth 0.0725 of total weight, while the section that would carry it must be written from evidence that cannot ground sector-specific claims — and thin, hedged, repetitive prose is graded by insight (0.32) and readability (0.14), i.e. 0.46 of weight, on the downside. The expected-value sign is not obviously positive, and the repo has already run this experiment once under a different name.

7) BUNDLING TELL. The "NEW FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT" (Tier F / Tier I / PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER, relaxing the NEUTRAL-drop path in provenance_generator.py:2596-2621) has NOTHING to do with an outline addendum. It is boilerplate pasted from a different proposal. If it were actually shipped with this arm it would be a large, independent relaxation of strict_verify smuggled in under an outline flag, making the arm unattributable and the faithfulness delta unmeasured. Nothing in a scope-dimension section requires admitting a new class of sentence.

8) §-1.3 SELF-CONTRADICTION. "Include exactly ONE thematic body organized ALONG that dimension" is a FORCED section — a target — appended to a prompt that says three lines above it "NEVER pad to a target count and NEVER invent a facet with no supporting evidence." Two conflicting instructions in one system prompt; when the model obeys the new one it does precisely what the old one exists to prevent.
